tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":57971,"title":"[GeneratorAgent] Docs: Document `build_and_classify` return type and `generatedGuard` field in README","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.GeneratorAgent` **Service directory:** `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57957) by `@Copilot` ## What Changed PR #57957 documented the `TestResult` structured return type for the `run_tests` tool in README.md and CHANGELOG.md, by adding property names (`Success`, `ExitCode`, `Passed`, `Failed`, `Skipped`, `Total`, `Failures`, `Error`, `RawOutput`) inline in the MCP Tools table. This established a pattern of documenting tool return types in the table. ## Gaps Found - The `build_and_classify` tool's response shape is **not described** in the README — yet it returns a rich JSON object with 9 fields (including an optional `generatedGuard` sub-object), which is equally important for agents calling the tool to understand - The automatic `generatedGuard` behaviour — when a Generated/ snapshot exists, `build_and_classify` auto-verifies integrity and reverts any violations, returning the result in the `generatedGuard` field — is completely absent from the README - The `classify_errors` tool's inputs and response are also undescribed; it classifies a batch of raw build errors rather than running a build itself, but there's no indication of what JSON it returns 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/README.md` — **edit** In the MCP Tools table, update the `build_and_classify` row from: ```markdown | `build_and_classify` | Run `dotnet build`, parse output, and classify each error as deterministic or requires-reasoning | ``` to: ```markdown | `build_and_classify` | Run `dotnet build`, parse output, and classify each error as deterministic or requires-reasoning. Returns `success`, `exitCode`, `totalErrors`, `returnedErrors` (capped at 20), `deterministicCount`, `requiresReasoningCount`, `classifiedErrors` (list of classified error objects), `rawOutput`, optional `buildFailureHint` (when errors couldn't be parsed), and optional `generatedGuard` (when a Generated/ snapshot exists — auto-verifies integrity and reverts any violations). | ``` Update the `classify_errors` row from: ```markdown | `classify_errors` | Classify a batch of build errors against the deterministic fix registry | ``` to: ```markdown | `classify_errors` | Classify a batch of pre-parsed build errors against the deterministic fix registry without running a build. Returns a list of classified error objects, each with `errorCode`, `message`, `filePath`, `line`, `isDeterministic`, `toolName`, and `toolArgs`. | ``` Also add a new sub-section under **Key concepts** → after the MCP Tools table, to document the `generatedGuard` automatic behaviour: ```markdown ### Generated code integrity guard When `build_and_classify` is called and a Generated/ snapshot exists (taken via `snapshot_generated`), the tool automatically verifies that no files in `Generated/` were modified during the build-fix cycle. If violations are found, they are auto-reverted and the `generatedGuard` field in the response will contain: - `violated` — `true` if violations were detected - `message` — human-readable summary - `violations` — list of `{ relativePath, type }` objects describing each modified or added file This guard prevents accidental edits to generated code and runs transparently on every `build_and_classify` call after a snapshot is taken. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No snippet test files are needed for this documentation change. The README uses prose and table descriptions (not `#region Snippet:` blocks) to document the MCP tools. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). This change adds content within the existing Key concepts section only — no sections need to be added. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `dotnet format sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/` No snippet update or API export is needed since this is a tool package with no public API surface changes. ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24169696229/agentic_workflow) · ● 657.2K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57971","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-09T02:53:52Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T02:53:52Z"}

This established a pattern of documenting tool return types in the table. ## Gaps Found - The `build_and_classify` tool's response shape is **not described** in the README — yet it returns a rich JSON object with 9 fields (including an optional `generatedGuard` sub-object), which is equally important for agents calling the tool to understand - The automatic `generatedGuard` behaviour — when a Generated/ snapshot exists, `build_and_classify` auto-verifies integrity and reverts any violations, returning the result in the `generatedGuard` field — is completely absent from the README - The `classify_errors` tool's inputs and response are also undescribed; it classifies a batch of raw build errors rather than running a build itself, but there's no indication of what JSON it returns 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/README.md` — **edit** In the MCP Tools table, update the `build_and_classify` row from: ```markdown | `build_and_classify` | Run `dotnet build`, parse output, and classify each error as deterministic or requires-reasoning | ``` to: ```markdown | `build_and_classify` | Run `dotnet build`, parse output, and classify each error as deterministic or requires-reasoning. Returns `success`, `exitCode`, `totalErrors`, `returnedErrors` (capped at 20), `deterministicCount`, `requiresReasoningCount`, `classifiedErrors` (list of classified error objects), `rawOutput`, optional `buildFailureHint` (when errors couldn't be parsed), and optional `generatedGuard` (when a Generated/ snapshot exists — auto-verifies integrity and reverts any violations). | ``` Update the `classify_errors` row from: ```markdown | `classify_errors` | Classify a batch of build errors against the deterministic fix registry | ``` to: ```markdown | `classify_errors` | Classify a batch of pre-parsed build errors against the deterministic fix registry without running a build. Returns a list of classified error objects, each with `errorCode`, `message`, `filePath`, `line`, `isDeterministic`, `toolName`, and `toolArgs`. | ``` Also add a new sub-section under **Key concepts** → after the MCP Tools table, to document the `generatedGuard` automatic behaviour: ```markdown ### Generated code integrity guard When `build_and_classify` is called and a Generated/ snapshot exists (taken via `snapshot_generated`), the tool automatically verifies that no files in `Generated/` were modified during the build-fix cycle. If violations are found, they are auto-reverted and the `generatedGuard` field in the response will contain: - `violated` — `true` if violations were detected - `message` — human-readable summary - `violations` — list of `{ relativePath, type }` objects describing each modified or added file This guard prevents accidental edits to generated code and runs transparently on every `build_and_classify` call after a snapshot is taken. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No snippet test files are needed for this documentation change. The README uses prose and table descriptions (not `#region Snippet:` blocks) to document the MCP tools. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). This change adds content within the existing Key concepts section only — no sections need to be added. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `dotnet format sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/` No snippet update or API export is needed since this is a tool package with no public API surface changes. ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24169696229/agentic_workflow) · ● 657.2K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57971","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-09T02:53:52Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T02:53:52Z"}

{"total_count":30,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4228787321,"number":57967,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[Azure Management Generator] Docs: Document scope-based extension resource parameter ordering and complex parent path ValidateRe [Content truncated due to length]","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` **Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` **Triggered by:** PR #57934 by `@live1206` ## What Changed Two bug fixes were merged for the Azure Management SDK generator/emitter: 1. **Generator (`OperationContext.cs`):** Fixed a LIFO stack push-order bug in `BuildContextualParameterHierarchy`. For scope-based extension resources (e.g., `ChaosTarget`, `KubernetesClusterExtension`), the stack was producing reversed positional order, causing `Id.Name` and `Id.ResourceType.Type` to be swapped in REST call arguments. 2. **Emitter (`resource-metadata.ts`):** Fixed `getExpectedParentResourceType` to handle parent paths with more than 3 segments. For resources scoped under nested multi-provider paths (e.g., `managementGroups/{mgId}/subscriptions/{subId}/providers/Microsoft.Quota/...`), the function now falls back to computing the parent scope resource type from the last `/providers/` segment, enabling `ValidateResourceId` generation for those collections. ## Gaps Found - The `Extension Resources` section in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` (lines 162–168) is a two-sentence placeholder. It does not explain scope-based extension resources, how the `BuildContextualParameterHierarchy` LIFO stack resolves positional parameters for them, or the interaction between `Id.Name` and `Id.ResourceType.Type` for extension resource contexts. - There is no documentation describing how the emitter's `getExpectedParentResourceType` function computes the expected parent resource type for collection resources, the 3-segment rule, or the fallback to the last `/providers/` segment for multi-provider nested scopes. - There is no changelog entry for either fix in the `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` package — there is no `CHANGELOG.md` at all in that directory.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` (edit) Expand the **Extension Resources** section (currently lines ~162–168) to cover scope-based extension resources in detail. Replace the current terse section with: ````markdown ### Extension Resources Extension resources have a flexible scope — they can be attached to any Azure resource. Their contextual path starts with a single `resourceUri` variable: ``` /{resourceUri}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName} ``` The `resourceUri` parameter is treated as a contextual parameter derived from the parent resource's `Id`. #### Scope-Based Extension Resources Some extension resources also capture the parent resource **type** as a contextual parameter in addition to its name. For example, a `ChaosTarget` contextual path looks like: ``` /{scope}/providers/Microsoft.Chaos/targets/{targetName} ```` When the generator builds `ContextualParameter` objects for such resources, each variable segment in the path is paired with the constant key that precedes it using a LIFO (stack-based) algorithm. The variable pairs are pushed onto the stack in **key-then-value** order so that the resulting positional list matches the path order from outermost to innermost scope. The generated REST call correctly supplies arguments in this order: ````csharp _restClient.Get( Id.Parent.ResourceType.Type, // e.g., "Microsoft.Kubernetes/connectedClusters" Id.Parent.Name, // e.g., "myCluster" Id.Name, // e.g., "myTarget" cancellationToken ); ``` If the stack push order were reversed (a previous bug), `Id.Name` and `Id.ResourceType.Type` would be swapped, producing incorrect REST calls. ```` #### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` (edit) Add a new subsection under **Advanced Scenarios** documenting the emitter's `ValidateResourceId` generation for complex parent paths. Add after the **Singleton Resources** subsection: ````markdown ### ValidateResourceId for Complex Multi-Provider Scopes When generating a resource collection, the emitter computes the **expected parent resource type** to emit a `ValidateResourceId` guard in the `GetAll` method. For simple paths (≤ 3 segments), the parent type is derived directly from the second-to-last provider segment. For nested multi-provider paths with more than 3 segments — such as: ``` managementGroups/{mgId}/subscriptions/{subId}/providers/Microsoft.Quota/quotas ``` the emitter falls back to computing the parent scope resource type from the **last `/providers/`** segment in the path. This ensures that `ValidateResourceId` is emitted correctly for these collection types even when the parent path has complex, multi-level scope nesting. ```` #### File 3: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/CHANGELOG.md` (create) Create a new CHANGELOG file to track generator/emitter changes: ```markdown # Changelog ## Unreleased ### Bug Fixes - Fixed incorrect parameter ordering for scope-based extension resources (e.g., `ChaosTarget`, `KubernetesClusterExtension`). The LIFO stack in `BuildContextualParameterHierarchy` was reversed, swapping `Id.Name` and `Id.ResourceType.Type` in generated REST client arguments. - Fixed `ValidateResourceId` generation for resource collections whose parent paths contain more than 3 segments (nested multi-provider scopes such as `managementGroups/{mgId}/subscriptions/{subId}/providers/Microsoft.Quota/...`). The emitter now falls back to the last `/providers/` segment to compute the expected parent resource type. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No README snippet placeholders are needed for these documentation-only changes (the docs are in `.md` files under `docs/`, not in a package README that uses the snippet generation tooling). ### Step 3: Verify README structure The `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is an npm package README (not a client library README), so the standard section checklist (Getting Started, Key Concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, etc.) is not required. No changes needed to `README.md`. ### Step 4: Validate The docs changes are Markdown-only; no build or test commands are required. However, if any `.cs` source was touched, run: 1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/` 2. `dotnet test eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Docs: Document redirect policy behavior and complete Examples section in README","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger` **Service directory:** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] by `@ryazhang-microsoft` ## What Changed PR #57869 significantly refactored `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` to cache the primary node URL from redirect responses and reuse it for subsequent non-GET requests. This improves write operation performance on multi-node ACL clusters. The policy was originally introduced in 1.4.1-beta.3 and improved in 1.4.1-beta.5. Neither the original introduction nor the caching improvement is reflected in the README. ## Gaps Found - **`## Examples` section says "Coming Soon..."** — despite the `Key concepts` section already containing inline code snippets, there is no organized Examples section with runnable, snippet-backed samples - **`ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` is undocumented** — the automatic 307/308 redirect following (with Authorization header preservation) is a significant behavioral feature that users migrating from earlier versions or other SDKs need to know about; no README section explains this - **`VerifyConnection` default change is undocumented** — beta.4 changed the default for `VerifyConnection` in `ConfidentialLedgerClientOptions` from `false` to `true`; this breaking-by-intent change has no README guidance - **API reference documentation link is commented out** — `` is commented out in the header badge row
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/README.md` — **edit** #### 1a. Add a "Redirect handling" subsection under `Key concepts` Insert the following after the `### Confidential consortium and enclave verifications` section and before `### Thread safety`: ```markdown ### Redirect handling Azure Confidential Ledger is a distributed system with multiple nodes. Write operations (non-GET requests) must be routed to the primary node; a non-primary node returns an HTTP 307/308 redirect to the current primary. The SDK automatically handles these redirects while preserving the `Authorization` header (which standard HTTP clients strip on cross-domain redirects). For efficiency, the SDK caches the most recently observed primary node URL and uses it as the first target for subsequent write requests, reducing the number of redirects experienced during steady-state operation. The cache is refreshed automatically whenever the service redirects to a new primary node. This behavior is built into `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy`, which is added to the pipeline by default. No extra configuration is required. ``` #### 1b. Replace the `## Examples` placeholder Replace: ```markdown ## Examples Coming Soon... ``` With: ```markdown ## Examples ### Append an entry and verify it was committed ```C# Snippet:AppendToLedger Operation postOperation = ledgerClient.PostLedgerEntry( waitUntil: WaitUntil.Completed, RequestContent.Create( new { contents = "Hello world!" })); string transactionId = postOperation.Id; Console.WriteLine($"Appended transaction with Id: {transactionId}"); ``` ### Check transaction status ```C# Snippet:GetStatus Response statusResponse = ledgerClient.GetTransactionStatus(transactionId); string status = JsonDocument.Parse(statusResponse.Content) .RootElement .GetProperty("state") .GetString(); Console.WriteLine($"Transaction status: {status}"); // Wait for the entry to be committed while (status == "Pending") { statusResponse = ledgerClient.GetTransactionStatus(transactionId); status = JsonDocument.Parse(statusResponse.Content) .RootElement .GetProperty("state") .GetString(); } Console.WriteLine($"Transaction status: {status}"); ``` ### Get a cryptographic receipt ```C# Snippet:GetReceipt Response receiptResponse = ledgerClient.GetReceipt(transactionId); string receiptJson = new StreamReader(receiptResponse.ContentStream).ReadToEnd(); Console.WriteLine(receiptJson); ``` ``` > **Note:** The snippet names (`AppendToLedger`, `GetStatus`, `GetReceipt`) are already defined in the existing Key concepts section. Confirm the snippet test file exists at `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/samples/` — if the `#region Snippet:` tags are present there, the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tool will fill in the code automatically. If not, the snippet regions should be added to the test file. #### 1c. Document `VerifyConnection` default in the "Authenticate the client" section Add under `#### Using Azure Active Directory`: ```markdown > **Note:** By default, `ConfidentialLedgerClientOptions.VerifyConnection` is `true`, which means the client validates the TLS certificate of the ledger node it connects to. Set this to `false` only in test or development environments. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The snippets `AppendToLedger`, `GetStatus`, `GetReceipt` should already exist in the Key concepts section of the README and in a test/sample file. Verify: 1. Check `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/samples/` for existing `#region Snippet:` tags for `AppendToLedger`, `GetStatus`, and `GetReceipt`. 2. If not present, create `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` with the sample code wrapped in `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` blocks. 3. Add the snippet placeholders in README under `## Examples` as shown above. 4. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 confidentialledger` to inject the code. Reference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/README.md` must contain these sections in this order. Current state: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started / Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started / Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started / Authenticate the client | ✅ Present (minor gap: no VerifyConnection note) | | Key concepts | ✅ Present (but missing redirect policy) | | Examples | ❌ "Coming Soon..." — needs real content | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ✅ Present | | Contributing | ✅ Present | ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 confidentialledger` — confirms snippets are injected from test files into README 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 confidentialledger` — no new public types were added, but run to confirm API listings are current 5. `dotnet format sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Docs: Remove orphaned ToC entry in Secrets MigrationGuide after sharelink retirement","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets` **Service directory:** `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/` **Triggered by:** PR #57907 by `@jsquire` ## What Changed PR #57907 retired the `sharelink` stand-alone sample (deprecated KV-managed storage accounts feature) and updated stale links in `MigrationGuide.md`. However, the Table of Contents entry for "Managing shared access signatures" was left in place despite the corresponding section body being absent from the document. ## Gaps Found - `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/MigrationGuide.md` contains a Table of Contents link `[Managing shared access signatures](#managing-shared-access-signatures)` (line 19) that points to a section that does not exist in the document body. This creates a broken anchor link and misleads readers into expecting documentation for a deprecated feature. - No mention anywhere in the guide that the KV-managed storage accounts / SAS feature has been deprecated, which could confuse users migrating from `Microsoft.Azure.KeyVault` who used that feature.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/MigrationGuide.md` **Action:** Edit Two changes are needed in this file: **Change 1 — Remove the orphaned ToC entry** (around line 19): Remove this line from the Table of Contents: ```markdown - [Managing shared access signatures](#managing-shared-access-signatures) ``` **Change 2 — Add a deprecation note for managed storage / SAS** after the "Deleting secrets" section and before the "Additional samples" section. Insert the following new section: ```markdown ### Managing shared access signatures The Azure Key Vault managed storage accounts feature — including SAS definition management — has been [deprecated]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Microsoft recommends storing SAS tokens or storage account keys directly as Key Vault secrets instead of using the managed storage accounts feature. If you were previously using `KeyVaultClient` methods such as `SetSasDefinitionAsync`, `GetSasDefinitionAsync`, `GetSasDefinitionsAsync`, or `RegenerateStorageAccountKeyAsync` from `Microsoft.Azure.KeyVault`, you should migrate to managing your storage credentials manually and storing them as ordinary Key Vault secrets via `SecretClient.SetSecretAsync`. For more information, see [Manage storage account keys with Key Vault and the Azure CLI (legacy)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet infrastructure is needed for this change — the new section is prose-only with no embedded code samples. ### Step 3: Verify README structure No README changes are required; this is a MigrationGuide-only fix. Verify `MigrationGuide.md` sections remain in correct order: 1. Migration benefits ✅ 2. General changes ✅ - Package and namespaces - Separate clients - Client constructors - Setting secrets - Getting secrets - Listing secrets - Listing secret versions - Deleting secrets - **Managing shared access signatures** ← new prose section to add 3. Additional samples ✅ 4. Support ✅ ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 keyvault` — confirm no README snippet regressions 4. `dotnet format sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > arguments.body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-generated issue documents gaps in the `Azure.GeneratorAgent` README — the `build_and_classify` and `classify_errors` tool return types are undescribed, and the automatic `generatedGuard` integrity behavior is absent from the documentation.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.GeneratorAgent` (sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/)
**Affected API:** `build_and_classify` tool, `classify_errors` tool, `generatedGuard` feature
**Scenarios:**
- `build_and_classify` response shape (9 fields including optional `generatedGuard` sub-object) is not documented in README
- The automatic `generatedGuard` behavior (auto-verify and revert Generated/ violations) is completely undescribed
- `classify_errors` tool inputs and response format are also undocumented

**Root ask:** Update `README.md` to add return type descriptions for `build_and_classify` and `classify_errors` in the MCP Tools table, and add a new "Generated code integrity guard" sub-section documenting the `generatedGuard` behavior.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue filed by an automated bot after PR #57957 established a pattern of documenting tool return types in the MCP Tools table. No code defects are reported.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #57957 to understand the established pattern for documenting tool return types inline in the MCP Tools table
2. Inspect `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/README.md` to confirm the current state of the `build_and_classify` and `classify_errors` rows
3. Implement the changes per the Implementation Guide in the issue body (update two table rows and add the `generatedGuard` sub-section)
4. Validate with `dotnet build sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/` and `dotnet test sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`
</details>

<details>
